title: Fix Heapify Error with Incorrect Comparison

statement.markdown: Given a binary heap of elements, perform a heapify operation on a subtree rooted at a given index. The heap is represented as an array, and the heapify operation ensures that the subtree rooted at the given index is a valid heap. The operation starts by checking if the given index is valid, and then recursively checks the children of the root node. If the root node's value is greater than either of its children's values, it swaps the root node with the child node with the smaller value. This process continues until the subtree is a valid heap. However, there is an error in the implementation that leads to incorrect results. Your task is to fix this error.
statement.inputFormat: An array of elements representing the binary heap and an integer index denoting the root of the subtree.
statement.outputFormat: The modified array representing the binary heap after the heapify operation.
statement.notes: The heap is a complete binary tree, where each parent node is either greater than (max-heap) or less than (min-heap) its child nodes.

constraints.description: Index of the root of the subtree

examples.explanation: The binary heap is initially represented as [3, 2, 13, 1, 4]. After performing the heapify operation on the subtree rooted at index 1, the modified heap is [1, 2, 13, 3, 4].

editorial.title: Heapify Operation
editorial.intuition: The heapify operation ensures that the subtree rooted at the given index is a valid heap by recursively checking the children of the root node.
editorial.bruteForce: The brute force approach involves recursively checking all nodes in the subtree and swapping the root node with the child node if necessary.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution uses a recursive approach to check the children of the root node and swap the root node with the child node if necessary.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The correctness of the solution can be proven by induction on the height of the subtree.
editorial.dryRun: The dry run involves manually applying the heapify operation on the given input to verify the correctness of the solution.
commonMistakes: Incorrect comparison between parent and child nodes

functionSignature.cpp: void heapify(vector<int>& nums, int n, int i);
functionSignature.java: void heapify(int[] nums, int n, int i);
functionSignature.python: def heapify(self, nums: list[int], n: int, i: int) -> None:
